name: gemini
description: Use when the user asks to run Gemini CLI for code review, plan review, or big context (>200k) processing. Ideal for comprehensive analysis requiring large context windows. Uses Gemini 3 Pro by default for state-of-the-art reasoning and coding.
capabilities: Execute Gemini CLI commands for code review across entire codebases
Perform architectural plan and technical specification analysis
Process contexts up to 1 million tokens for comprehensive codebase understanding
Support multiple Gemini models (gemini-3-pro-preview, gemini-3-flash, legacy 2.5 variants)
Provide background execution safety with proper approval mode configuration
Generate structured analysis reports for security, performance, and code quality
author: softaworks
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing no executable code. Static scanner detected patterns in markdown code blocks (shell command examples) and documentation URLs, all of which are false positives. Safe for marketplace publication.
valueStatement: Large codebases and complex analysis tasks exceed the context limits of many AI models. This skill enables Claude Code to leverage Google Gemini CLI for comprehensive code review, architectural analysis, and processing contexts up to 1 million tokens.
userTitle: Run Gemini CLI for code review and analysis
limitations: Requires Gemini CLI v0.16.0 or later to be installed separately
Requires Google Cloud credentials configured for API access
Cannot resume interrupted sessions (Gemini CLI is primarily one-shot or interactive)
Does not include built-in multi-modal image analysis capabilities

useCases.targetUser: Security engineers and developers conducting thorough security audits
useCases.title: Comprehensive Security Code Review
useCases.description: Run automated security analysis across entire codebases, identifying vulnerabilities, OWASP Top 10 issues, and security anti-patterns using Gemini 3 Pro state-of-the-art reasoning.

useCases.targetUser: Technical architects and team leads reviewing proposed solutions
useCases.title: Architectural Plan Analysis
useCases.description: Analyze technical specifications, system designs, and project roadmaps for scalability concerns, missing components, and integration challenges.

useCases.targetUser: Developers onboarding to new projects or legacy system analysts
useCases.title: Large Codebase Understanding
useCases.description: Process and analyze entire codebases with up to 1 million tokens of context to understand architecture, patterns, technical debt, and refactoring opportunities.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Code Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review the codebase focusing on security vulnerabilities, performance issues, and code quality. Report findings with severity levels and remediation suggestions.

promptTemplates.title: Security-Focused Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Perform a security audit checking for: OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities, JWT token handling security, session management, rate limiting, password hashing strength, and injection vulnerabilities.

promptTemplates.title: Full Codebase Architecture Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze the entire codebase to understand: overall architecture, key patterns and conventions, potential technical debt, dependency cycles, refactoring opportunities, and code organization.

promptTemplates.title: Plan and Specification Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this architectural plan for: scalability concerns, missing components, integration challenges, single points of failure, alternative approaches, and potential bottlenecks.

bestPractices: Always use `--approval-mode yolo` for background or automated tasks to prevent hung processes
Default to `gemini-3-pro-preview` for best analysis quality (35% better at software engineering)
Use timeout wrapper (`timeout 300 gemini ...`) for safety in automated environments
antiPatterns: Never use `--approval-mode default` in background or non-interactive shells (causes indefinite hangs)
Avoid running without specifying model explicitly (may use suboptimal legacy versions)
Do not skip timeout wrappers for long-running analysis tasks

faq.question: Which Gemini model should I use?
faq.answer: Default to `gemini-3-pro-preview` for best quality (76.2% on SWE-bench). Use `gemini-3-flash` for speed-critical tasks requiring sub-second latency. Legacy 2.5 models are available for cost-sensitive scenarios.

faq.question: Why does Gemini hang in background mode?
faq.answer: Using `--approval-mode default` in non-interactive shells causes Gemini to wait indefinitely for user approval prompts. Always use `--approval-mode yolo` or add timeout wrappers for background tasks.

faq.question: How much context can Gemini process?
faq.answer: Gemini 3 models support up to 1 million tokens of input with 64k output. This enables analysis of entire codebases in a single request.

faq.question: Do I need to install Gemini CLI separately?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill requires Gemini CLI v0.16.0 or later to be installed and configured with Google Cloud credentials. Install via: npm install -g @google/gemini-cli

faq.question: Can I resume an interrupted Gemini session?
faq.answer: No. Gemini CLI sessions are primarily one-shot or interactive. Unlike Codex, there is no built-in resume functionality. For follow-up analysis, start a new session with context from previous findings.

faq.question: What approval modes are available?
faq.answer: Three modes: `default` (interactive approval prompts), `auto_edit` (auto-approves edit tools only), and `yolo` (auto-approves all tools). Use `yolo` for background/automated tasks.
